other_observers: Duane Nelson, Tony Leukering, Mark Peterson, Joe Roller, Glenn Walbek, Norm Erthal; Lance Verhoeff - landowner
specific_location: west side of Bent County Road 26.5, between US 50 and County Road JJ at the west edge of an alfalfa field
description: Long-legged bird with huge bill and raptor-like appearance. Black cap that extended down to the nape. Bill huge and yellow orange with noticeable hooked tip. White cheeks extending down to upper breast where fine dark vertical streaks extended to all dark belly. Very long legs were yellow orange. When preeing could see some white towards the tip of the underwings.Behaviors: Perched about 3 feet off of the ground. Preening for over 20 minutes.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
